Hey, what's going on? Okay, so let's get right to it. These sales FAQ. Okay, someone says, hey, what's the membership price? This is something that you don't want to do with this in the initial consultation because you don't want to sell two things at once. You want to just sell this sale and then sell them on how great you are while you're fulfilling. So what's the price? Be like, don't worry about the price. It's less than the challenge. It won't get you a great deal. Let's just get you started. I'll just give you great results when I come in, what I just said. Just sell them on the actual sale that you're focused on. Tell them that it's going to be cheaper than the challenge going forward, but don't worry about that right now. Let's just get you great results. That sound good? Great. Can I try a session first? I don't really get these a lot. Some people do, but it's really, really rare. It means that you haven't really sold them on the end result because they shouldn't even be thinking about workouts or meal plans or anything because they should be focusing on the end goal or the vacation of where they're trying to go. When someone's like, can I try a session first? It's like, no, everyone starts in two weeks and don't worry because at the end of the day, and this is the overcome that we would give them, at the end of the day, is it going to be a long walk on the beach with the wind in your hair? No, it's not. You're going to be lifting weights, you're going to be doing cardio, you're going to be sore, but it's going to be exactly what you need to get the results you're looking for. That's it. It's not going to be like you're walking on the beach with the wind in your hair. It's not going to be like fun, zoom, but dancing, but what it will be is going to be exactly what you need to get the results you're looking for. That's it because then it shouldn't be focused on, well, I like the sessions, it should be focused on what result am I going to be getting because that's the whole point. How much should my sales rate be post-challenge? 150 to 199 is your effective rate after the discount should be where you should be aiming, and that should be for a 12 month commitment. Money trick with IDs, and they give you your ID and you can see that they have credit cards in there. Then after you write all the info that they have from their ID, then you say, hey, let me trade you. What card do you want to use? You can point at their wallet. What card do you want to use? Then they'll swap it with you because now they can't say they forgot their wallet at home or whatever. They're like, well, I don't have the card that I want to use, but like, cool, we'll put this one on and you can call me to switch it out tomorrow. We'll put the first billing for tomorrow in that way. You can call me beforehand and swap it out otherwise it will run in this card, but that way you won't forget. That way I can reserve the spot for you. Works fine. The money trick with the ID is a great way to get them to take their wallet out. It's like an escalation. You want to do it? Yes. Okay, cool. Do you have an ID on you? Cool. That's like an intermediary. Okay, cool. It may have your card, so it's like you're stepping, you're warming them up and it works well. Some people start because they don't believe that they can sell it, but I promise you that the limiting beliefs are only in your mind, not in the mind of your prospects. All right, we've sold it at $5.99 in the absolute ghetto. You'll be fine. What's the bet for bulking? 15 pounds of actual weight gain in six weeks is a good bet for bulking. I've done it for women and men. For women, I might do 10 pounds. For men, I'll do 15 or 20. Objective numbers to give you, but that's just 15 to 20-ish and 10 if it's a really small girl. It looks like 100 pounds and wants to put on weight. 10 pounds of scale weight for that girl is going to be 10% increase. So it's going to be significant. I guess it probably is about 10%. So 10% increase in weight. So you go from 100 to 110. If you're 150, you go to 165. If you're 200, you go to 220. It's an easy way to do it. How do I get less partial payments? You decide to get less partial payments. That's pretty much what I did with my whole sales team. Didn't decrease our sales whatsoever. Have a hard line there because what happens if you make all these crazy payment plans and collections becomes a nightmare and then you feel like you're going to make money and then all you're doing is dealing with declines all day and you don't want to do with that. So half before the stack of the contracts that I actually had filled out. So maybe we have 25 at this point, but they're all filled out. I'd stack those on top of the fresh printouts and I'd only have like three or four printouts that were blank on the table. So when I'd say like, listen, I don't know if we have a lot, if we have that many spots left, I would put my hand on or I point towards the stack of contracts and they're like, holy cow. Can we over deliver? Great. Awesome. Accountability. And then you go like, there's three things we're going to do. You have your own coach. Okay. The coach is going to reach out to you every week just to check in with you, make sure that you're doing okay. You have a group of online support of all the people who've done this program in the past. So that way you have a benefit of the people who've done in the past, but also people doing it with you. So the people who were like, oh my God, my legs are on fire, but you also have the encouragement of past alumni. Does that sound good? Does that make sense to you? Okay, cool. So it doesn't sound like the most amazing program of all time. Like, they're going to be, you don't need to dive really deep on here because then that gives them more things to say no to. Well, tell me more about how the workouts are structured. Tell me more about how this meal plan works. That's called an analyst frame and they're trying to break your momentum by asking details so that they can find a detail they can say no to. So when they say like meal plans, they're like, listen, you're going to be more protein, you're going to be less carbs, you're going to be less fat. All the stuff you know that you should be doing, you're going to be doing that and you're just going to be doing it consistently because you're going to put money on the line. Right? Boom. Right? So that way you can overcome these little interjections. Okay? How do I do a group pitch? I have a recording of a group pitch. Just watch it. That's the easiest way to do it. How do I deal with multiple people showing up? If you have three or less, you can deal with just like a one-on-one. Go through all the pain, see if you can bring some collaboration between the people. That's what you do there. If it's like four or more, then it really becomes a pitch and then you have to start mentally dealing with the obstacle overcomes that would typically come up during the pitch. That was the best success rate that we had when we were running the challenge. It varied. I just went off the best success percentage because it was compelling. But I'd say we still averaged like across the board plus 70% like every time, like just about every time. So you should be fine. Like you just need to make sure that they're following the plan. And so as long as you do your job and make sure that they follow the plan, if you don't follow up with them about following the plan, they're not going to follow it. So you need to have the conviction that they don't have. So I'm going to ask you to do something which is a big ask. You need to trust me. This plan works. We've done it a bajillion times. You just need to tell them to follow it and you need to believe in them the moment that they have a question about it. So you're going to look to you and if you stutter, you hesitate, they're going to take another step forward and say, this doesn't work. All right. So you have to come back and be like, you're not following it. Okay. Like I'm just telling you 99 times out of 100, that's the same in the garlic. How can we still make it free to you but still get you the result you're looking for? So we put this deposit in place. Everyone's results went through the roof and then we actually serve our customers. It happens all the time. We've been doing it for years. So yes, it's totally safe. Another question is, is it safe to be 250 pounds? Not really. Right. So if you, I would ask them, like, what particular part would you find unsafe? Like, I don't know. I feel like I've heard that. Right. Well, we've also heard that you can't eat carbs. I've heard that you should eat like bacon is not good for you. That stuff changes all the time. But reality is most people are going to eat a lot of food right before they weigh in. So they're going to be temporarily bloated and have a lot of, you know, water stores, a little bit of the gamification and we get that we built that into the challenge. So that's how we know that what you are losing, though, if even if you game it a little bit, you're going to lose 10 real pounds of tissue, 12 real pounds of tissue over this 12 over these six weeks, which is one to two pounds, which is totally, totally normal. Right. And they're like, okay, I get it. That's why they're like, because if it were a 10 pound challenge, you could just eat a lot tomorrow and then not do anything and then, you know, run with a trash bag on the last day and then you lose 10 pounds. That make sense? Yeah. Okay, cool. Sitting arrangements. A lot of people ask me how what's the best way to like actually sit in these sales consultations? You want to have a table there because you have to fill out stuff. I love having lots of clipboards. I'm like a clipboard feed. I love clipboards. So I have, I feel like there's always uses for clipboards. And so I always have like four or five clipboards in my sales room in addition to the ones that are on the walls. So I use those clipboards. I have first contracts on them so that I can just hand them to people or I can fill them out and then hand them to them to fill out the credit card stuff or whatever. If it's multiple people coming in, if it's one on one, I usually fill out the whole contract for them so I know that it's filled out right. But in terms of seating arrangements, I never sit across from a prospect very rarely. I can't even think of a time that I would sit across from a prospect because then it feels like a sale. And so normally I'll just have someone come in and I'll just pull up a chair for them and I'll pull up a chair for me and I'll just sit basically like kitty corner like this. I'll sit like this with them. So like I'm facing them but like I'm not like directly facing them but like more like this. So I can just talk to them like we're sitting buddy to buddy shoulder to shoulder that way we feel like we're on the same team. And it's not like me against you, it's me with you seeing if I can get you to the finish line. Okay, I think it's a subconscious thing but I personally I never sat across from them. They just feel like it's a sale so they get defensive. Even when I would do group pitches what I would do is if I had a table there and I had a bunch of chairs I would sit on the table. I would sit on my stand, sit on my stich, just kind of put my butt on the table and lean on it so it was casual. So it was just more of like a hey we're talking about this as presentation rather than is something whatever. I don't want it to feel like a pitch. Actually one of my best, one of the acquaintance of mine who was one of the best sales guys at a Toyota dealership he outsold like everyone like three to one and he would just close people in the lobby. He would just sit down with them on the couch and chat with them rather than bring them to the desk. So they just had their guards down. That was the whole point. Okay, how do I politely eject a negative Nancy in a group sale? You say hey so right after you're like okay. And this is really key for any kind of group sales. When you get to the end you get to the pitch or you presented the offer like okay who wants one of the last spots or has some more questions before moving forward. That's the question and then you raise your hand as you say it. Okay, who wants one of the last spots or has a question behind you. If someone doesn't raise your hand you're like ma'am totally understand. I don't want to take any more of your time so I wish you the best of luck with all your fitness goals. Okay, and then I basically just stare at them until they move and then they'll usually leave and now I just had this group think of a small group you just answer any of the one off questions. What I need to think about it you should do it now otherwise you're not going to need more spots. What if blah blah blah blah like don't worry we have a money back guarantee on the fact that if we don't provide you excellent service we'll give you money back. I use that all the time and I never had anyone take me up on it. Just a satisfaction guarantee and it worked great. Okay, what if someone doesn't have either? What about a percentage of total pounds lost like 10% that's just not the challenge. People use like if someone comes in at 20% body fat into girl she can lose 5% body fat like you can get down to 15% like it's especially if someone hasn't worked out in a while. It's really rare that you're going to have someone who's like true great shape who walks in the door and if they do just sell them on a membership right or sell them a challenge that doesn't have the money back for $2.99 if they're just trying to get their butt kicked but like it's such a small percentage of people. We would usually sell 2 thirds on the weight loss 1 third on the body fat percentage and it worked great so I'd recommend doing that. The program is legal we've done it 300 times I've had multiple different legal firms look at it like we're fine there's nothing you have to worry about like you're good it's totally okay like you're fine.
